decisions.title: Login and Registration via SSO
decisions.detail: Users will log in exclusively via Single Sign-On (SSO) through the external Portal.

evidence.quote: Users will log in exclusively via SSO through the external Portal.
evidence.approximate_location: I. Core Features (V1 Implementation Scope) - Login / Registration

decisions.title: No Internal Login/Registration
decisions.detail: The system will not implement its own login or registration functionality.

evidence.quote: This system will not implement its own login or registration functionality.
evidence.approximate_location: I. Core Features (V1 Implementation Scope) - Login / Registration

decisions.title: Prioritize Photo Upload and Output Modeling
decisions.detail: The two main features to prioritize for V1 are photo upload and output modeling.

evidence.quote: Two main features to prioritize:1. Photo upload2. Output modeling
evidence.approximate_location: I. Core Features (V1 Implementation Scope) - Core Functionality

decisions.title: System to Support Photo Upload
decisions.detail: The system must support photo upload as a core functionality.

evidence.approximate_location: I. Core Features (V1 Implementation Scope) - Core Functionality

decisions.title: System to Support Output Modeling
decisions.detail: The system must support output modeling as a core functionality.

evidence.approximate_location: I. Core Features (V1 Implementation Scope) - Core Functionality

decisions.title: Sidebar to Distinguish Folder Types
decisions.detail: The sidebar interface needs to distinguish between 'Album' and 'Project' folder types, managing photos and modeling data separately.

evidence.quote: The sidebar needs to distinguish between two folder types — "Album" and "Project" — managing photos and modeling data separately
evidence.approximate_location: I. Core Features (V1 Implementation Scope) - Interface & Interaction

decisions.title: Album Functionality: Add, Delete, Search Photos
decisions.detail: The Album functionality must support adding, deleting, and searching photos.

evidence.quote: The Album must support adding, deleting, and searching photos
evidence.approximate_location: I. Core Features (V1 Implementation Scope) - Interface & Interaction

decisions.title: Modeling Progress Bar Display
decisions.detail: Modeling progress must be shown as a progress bar, displaying the status of each stage (e.g., AT computing, point cloud generation, model generation).

evidence.quote: Modeling progress must be shown as a progress bar, displaying the status of each stage (AT computing, point cloud generation, model generation, etc.)
evidence.approximate_location: I. Core Features (V1 Implementation Scope) - Interface & Interaction

decisions.title: Project Creation Photo Selection
decisions.detail: When creating a project, users must be able to select photos from their photo list to import into the project folder.

evidence.quote: When creating a project, users can select photos from the photo list to import into the project folder
evidence.approximate_location: I. Core Features (V1 Implementation Scope) - Interface & Interaction

decisions.title: Future Feature: Project Sharing
decisions.detail: A project folder should allow designating other users to jointly view modeling results in a future version.

evidence.quote: Project sharing: A project folder can designate other users to jointly view the modeling results

decisions.title: Album Privacy (Owner Only)
decisions.detail: Albums are private to the owner only and will not support multi-user access.

evidence.quote: Albums are private to the owner only and do not support multi-user access

decisions.title: Future Feature: Resumable Uploads
decisions.detail: In a future version, the system should support resumable uploads where upload progress is preserved via a caching mechanism if an upload is interrupted.

evidence.quote: Resumable uploads: If an upload is interrupted, upload progress is preserved via a caching mechanism, supporting resume-from-breakpoint

decisions.title: Future Feature: Storage Quota Management
decisions.detail: In a future version, the user's personal page should display used and remaining storage, and each user's photo-upload limit can be dynamically adjusted based on system settings and permissions.

evidence.quote: Storage quota management: The user's personal page displays used and remaining storage; each user's photo-upload limit can be dynamically adjusted based on system settings and permissions

decisions.title: Future Feature: AT Error Handling
decisions.detail: In a future version, when AT computation fails, the system should provide a mechanism to adjust parameters and re-run.

evidence.quote: AT error handling: When AT computation fails, provide a mechanism to adjust parameters and re-run

decisions.title: Future Feature: Expert Mode
decisions.detail: In a future version, after photo upload, the system should distinguish between 'Normal Mode' (default parameters) and 'Expert Mode' (freely adjustable parameters).

evidence.quote: Expert mode: After photo upload, distinguish between "Normal Mode" (default parameters) and "Expert Mode" (freely adjustable parameters)

decisions.title: V1 Scope: Normal Mode Only
decisions.detail: For V1, only 'Normal Mode' (default parameters) will be implemented.

evidence.quote: V1 will implement Normal Mode only

implementation_scope: Integrate with external Single Sign-On (SSO) Portal for user authentication and authorization (D001).
Develop photo upload system, including client-side and server-side components (D004).
Develop output modeling system, including backend processing for AT computing, point cloud generation, and model generation (D005).
Implement user interface sidebar to differentiate and manage 'Album' and 'Project' folder types, handling photos and modeling data separately (D006).
Implement Album functionality to add, delete, and search photos (D007).
Implement UI component for a modeling progress bar, displaying status for each stage of the modeling process (D008).
Develop functionality for creating a project, allowing users to select and import photos from their photo list into the project folder (D009).
Implement 'Normal Mode' for output modeling using default parameters (D016).
Enforce album privacy: restrict access to the owner only (D011).

constraints.text: User authentication and registration must be handled exclusively by an external SSO Portal; no internal login/registration system is to be built.
Album access must be restricted to the owner only; multi-user access for albums is not permitted.

architectural_decisions.text: Adopt an external Single Sign-On (SSO) system as the sole authentication mechanism, precluding any internal user credential management.
For V1, restrict output modeling functionality to 'Normal Mode' with default parameters only, deferring 'Expert Mode' to future versions.

open_technical_questions: What is the specific API/protocol for integrating with the external SSO Portal (e.g., OAuth2, OpenID Connect) and what user data will be provided?
What are the technical requirements for photo upload (e.g., max file size, supported formats, client-side preprocessing, storage solution, direct upload vs. proxied)?
What is the detailed backend architecture for output modeling (e.g., microservices, job queueing, specific libraries/algorithms for AT computing, point cloud generation, model generation)?
How will modeling progress status (AT computing, point cloud generation, model generation) be communicated from the backend services to the frontend in real-time?
What are the precise 'default parameters' for 'Normal Mode' modeling, and how will they be configured and applied?
What data structures and database schema design are needed to differentiate and manage 'Album' and 'Project' folder types and their associated data (photos vs. modeling data)?
What is the chosen technical approach for 'searching photos' within an Album (e.g., metadata indexing, full-text search engine integration, database query optimization)?
How will user identity from the external SSO be mapped to an internal user representation for managing ownership and access control of Albums and Projects?

acceptance_criteria: Given a user attempts to log in, When they are redirected to the external SSO Portal, Then successful authentication on the external Portal grants access to the system.
The system's user interface must not contain any internal login forms or registration links.
The system must allow users to upload photos.
The system must allow users to initiate output modeling.
The sidebar interface must visually distinguish between 'Album' and 'Project' folder types.
The sidebar interface must allow separate management of photos within 'Album' folders and modeling data within 'Project' folders.
Given a user is in an 'Album' folder, When they select photos to add, Then the selected photos are successfully added to the 'Album'.
Given a user is in an 'Album' folder, When they select one or more photos and choose to delete them, Then the selected photos are successfully removed from the 'Album'.
Given a user is in an 'Album' folder, When they use the search functionality for photos, Then the system displays photos matching the search criteria.

Given a modeling process is initiated, When the modeling process starts, Then a progress bar is displayed showing the status of each stage (e.g., AT computing, point cloud generation, model generation).
Given a user is creating a new 'Project', When they access the photo selection interface, Then they can view and select photos from their available photo list.
Given a user has selected photos during 'Project' creation, When they confirm the selection, Then the chosen photos are successfully imported into the new 'Project' folder.
Given an 'Album' is created by a specific user, When another user attempts to view or access that 'Album', Then access is denied.
The system must only present options for 'Normal Mode' modeling (default parameters) and not display any 'Expert Mode' adjustable parameters for V1.
test_scenarios: Verify a user can successfully log in to the system via the external SSO Portal.
Attempt to navigate to any URL or UI element that might suggest an internal login or registration process and confirm none exist.
Upload a single photo to an 'Album' folder and verify its successful display.
Upload multiple photos to an 'Album' folder and verify their successful display.
Create a new 'Album' folder and verify it appears correctly in the sidebar.
Create a new 'Project' folder and verify it appears correctly in the sidebar, distinct from 'Album' folders.
Delete a single photo from an 'Album' and verify it is removed.
Delete multiple photos from an 'Album' and verify they are all removed.
Search for an existing photo within an 'Album' by its filename or metadata.
Search for a non-existent photo within an 'Album' and verify no results are returned.
Start a modeling process for a 'Project' and observe the progress bar updating through its various stages.
Create a new 'Project', select several photos from an existing 'Album', and confirm they are imported into the 'Project'.
Log in as User A, create an 'Album'. Log in as User B, attempt to access User A's album and confirm access is denied.
Verify that after photo upload and initiating modeling, there are no options presented for 'Expert Mode' parameter adjustments.
edge_cases: Attempt to log in via SSO with invalid credentials on the external Portal and observe system behavior (e.g., error message, redirect).
Attempt to upload a photo with an unsupported file type.
Attempt to upload a photo that exceeds the maximum allowed file size.
Attempt to upload a very large number of photos in a single batch (e.g., 1000+).
Attempt to delete photos from an 'Album' while they are currently being used in an active 'Project' modeling process (if applicable).
Search for photos using special characters or an empty search query.
Initiate modeling for a 'Project' with a minimal number of photos (e.g., 1-2).
Initiate modeling for a 'Project' with a very large number of photos.
Observe progress bar behavior if a modeling stage completes unusually quickly or slowly.
As a non-owner user, attempt to find a direct link or path to another user's private 'Album' and verify access is blocked.
unresolved_behaviors: What is the expected system behavior when SSO authentication fails or the external Portal is unavailable? (Owner: PM/QA)
What are the supported image file types for photo uploads? (Owner: PM/QA)
What is the maximum file size limit for a single photo upload? (Owner: PM/QA)
What is the maximum number of photos that can be uploaded in a single batch? (Owner: PM/QA)
What are the specific stages to be displayed in the modeling progress bar for V1 (e.g., beyond 'AT computing, point cloud generation, model generation')? (Owner: PM/QA)
What is the expected behavior and UI feedback if a modeling process fails during V1 (before the 'AT error handling' feature is implemented)? (Owner: PM/QA)

decision_history: Decision D001: Users will log in exclusively via Single Sign-On (SSO) through the external Portal, thus D002: The system will not implement its own login or registration functionality.
Decision D003: The two main features prioritized for V1 are photo upload (D004) and output modeling (D005).
Decision D006: The sidebar interface in V1 will distinguish between 'Album' and 'Project' folder types, managing photos and modeling data separately.
Decision D007: Album functionality for V1 must support adding, deleting, and searching photos.
Decision D011: Albums in V1 will be private to the owner only and will not support multi-user access.
Decision D009: When creating a project, users must be able to select photos from their photo list to import into the project folder.
Decision D008: Modeling progress for V1 must be shown as a progress bar, displaying the status of each stage (e.g., AT computing, point cloud generation, model generation).
Decision D016: For V1, only 'Normal Mode' (default parameters) for photo modeling will be implemented, with 'Expert Mode' (freely adjustable parameters) deferred to a future version (D015).
Decision D010: Project sharing, D012: Resumable uploads, D013: Storage quota management, and D014: AT error handling are confirmed as future features beyond V1 scope.
dependencies: External Portal for Single Sign-On (SSO) integration (Confirmed). The system relies entirely on this external component for user authentication and login.
risks: High: Critical dependency on external SSO Portal for all user logins (D001, D002). Any issues or delays with the external Portal's SSO service will directly block user access to our system. (Impact: High, Likelihood: Medium - inherent risk of external dependency)

Medium: Scope creep risk from 'future features' (D010, D012, D013, D014, D015). While explicitly out of V1, these could create stakeholder expectation management challenges or pressure to include them prematurely.
open_questions: What are the specific API contracts, integration points, and security protocols required for integrating with the external SSO Portal? (Owned by PM, blocked by External Portal team/Engineer)
What are the acceptance criteria for 'Normal Mode' modeling (D016) and what are the expected default parameters? (Owned by PM/QA)

system_requirements: The system SHALL authenticate users exclusively via Single Sign-On (SSO) through the external Portal (D001).
The system MUST NOT implement its own user login or registration functionality (D002).
The system SHALL support the upload of user photos (D004).
The system SHALL support the generation of output models (D005).
The user interface sidebar SHALL visually distinguish between 'Album' and 'Project' folder types (D006).
The system SHALL manage photos within 'Album' folders (D006).
The system SHALL manage modeling data within 'Project' folders (D006).
The system SHALL allow users to add photos to an Album (D007).
The system SHALL allow users to delete photos from an Album (D007).
The system SHALL allow users to search for photos within an Album (D007).
The system SHALL display the progress of modeling tasks via a progress bar (D008).
The modeling progress bar SHALL display the status of each stage of the modeling process, including AT computing, point cloud generation, and model generation (D008).
The system SHALL allow users to select existing photos from their photo list for import into a Project folder during project creation (D009).
Albums SHALL be private to their owner only (D011).
The system MUST NOT support multi-user access to Albums (D011).
For V1, the system SHALL only process output modeling using 'Normal Mode' (default parameters) (D016).
The system SHALL support future functionality to allow designating other users to jointly view modeling results in a project folder (D010).
The system SHALL support future functionality for resumable uploads where upload progress is preserved via a caching mechanism if an upload is interrupted (D012).

The system SHALL support future functionality to display used and remaining storage on the user's personal page, and dynamically adjust user photo-upload limits based on system settings and permissions (D013).
The system SHALL support future functionality to provide a mechanism to adjust parameters and re-run AT computation when it fails (D014).
The system SHALL support future functionality to distinguish between 'Normal Mode' (default parameters) and 'Expert Mode' (freely adjustable parameters) for modeling after photo upload (D015).
data_flows: User authentication requests SHALL be redirected from this system to the external Portal's SSO system, which SHALL return an authentication token/session to this system (D001).
User-uploaded photo data SHALL flow from the client application to the system's storage component (D004).
Selected photo metadata or references from 'Album' storage SHALL be associated with or copied into 'Project' folders during project creation (D009).
Project photo data SHALL flow from storage to a modeling processing component for output modeling (D005).
Modeling progress updates (e.g., AT computing status, point cloud generation status, model generation status) SHALL flow from the modeling processing component to the system's user interface (D008).
Generated model output data SHALL flow from the modeling processing component to 'Project' storage (D005).
interface_contracts: SSO Integration (D001): The system SHALL integrate with the external Portal's SSO service. This implies an authentication redirect/callback mechanism. Expected input: Authentication token or session identifier from the SSO provider. Expected output: An authenticated user session within this system.

Photo Upload API (D004, D007): The system SHALL expose an endpoint for uploading photo files, likely supporting multipart/form-data. This API SHALL accept parameters for associating photos with an 'Album' (e.g., POST /api/albums/{albumId}/photos).

Photo Management API (D007): The system SHALL expose APIs for deleting photos (e.g., DELETE /api/albums/{albumId}/photos/{photoId}) and searching photos within an Album (e.g., GET /api/albums/{albumId}/photos?search={query}).

Project Creation API (D009): The system SHALL expose an API for creating new projects, allowing users to specify project details and provide a list of existing photo identifiers from their Albums to be imported (e.g., POST /api/projects, with payload including photo_ids).

Modeling Process API (D005, D008, D016): The system SHALL expose an API to initiate the modeling process for a given project (e.g., POST /api/projects/{projectId}/model/start). The system SHALL also expose an API to query the status of an ongoing modeling process, returning current stage and progress (e.g., GET /api/projects/{projectId}/model/status).
Folder Management API (D006): The system SHALL expose an API to retrieve a list of user's 'Album' and 'Project' folders (e.g., GET /api/folders).

constraints.text: User authentication MUST be handled exclusively by the external Portal's Single Sign-On (SSO) system.

architectural_decisions.text: The system will not implement its own user login or registration functionality, relying entirely on an external SSO provider.
For V1, output modeling functionality will be restricted to 'Normal Mode' using default parameters, with 'Expert Mode' deferred to a future version.

open_analysis_questions: What are the detailed technical specifications (e.g., specific protocol like OAuth2/OpenID Connect version, endpoint URLs, required scopes, token format) for integrating with the external Portal's SSO service?
What are the required metadata fields for uploaded photos (e.g., EXIF data extraction, user-defined tags) and how should they be stored and indexed?

What is the exact technical mechanism for 'importing' photos from an Album into a Project? Specifically, will photos be physically copied, symlinked, or will only their metadata/references be associated?
What are the functional and non-functional requirements for the modeling processing component, including specific algorithms, infrastructure requirements, and expected processing times (SLAs)?
What are the expected output data formats for the generated models (e.g., 3D mesh formats, point cloud formats)?
What are the maximum file size limits for photo uploads, and what are the performance expectations for upload speed and concurrency?
What are the specific data retention policies and backup strategies required for photos and modeling data?

stakeholder.business_summary: Our initial product launch (V1) will focus on two core features: allowing users to upload photos and then process those photos into 3D models. Users will access the system seamlessly through our existing external login portal, ensuring a consistent and secure experience without needing a new account or password. We've also outlined several advanced features for future releases to enhance collaboration and user control.
key_impact: Users will have a simple, secure login experience by using our existing company portal.
The product will deliver immediate value by enabling users to upload photos and generate 3D models, addressing key business needs.
The system is designed for future growth, with plans to introduce collaboration tools and advanced user controls in later versions.
User progress during modeling will be clearly visible, improving satisfaction and transparency.
Initial focus on a 'Normal Mode' ensures ease of use for the majority of users, with advanced customization (Expert Mode) planned for later.

review.reviewer_notes: All role-specific outputs (Engineer, QA, PM, Stakeholder) are consistent with the extracted decisions. No hallucinations, quality issues (vagueness, missing sections, contradictions), or sensitive content/prompt injection attempts were detected. The report is ready for publication.

This system will not implement its own login or registration functionality.
